title: Intellia Therapeutics Reports Positive Phase 3 Results For HAE Gene Therapy; Stock Up
text: Intellia Therapeutics reported positive Phase 3 results for its lonvo-z gene therapy candidate in hereditary angioedema, resulting in stock price appreciation. The global Phase 3 HAELO trial met its primary and all key secondary endpoints. The treatment showed an 87% reduction in mean monthly attacks (0.26 vs 2.1) versus placebo. 62% of lonvo-z patients were attack-free compared to 11% on placebo. The therapy is an in vivo CRISPR gene editing candidate intended to inactivate the kallikrein B1 (KLKB1) gene to permanently lower kallikrein and bradykinin levels. It is designed as a one-time treatment administered in an outpatient setting. Intellia began a rolling FDA BLA, targeting completion in 2H 2026 and a potential U.S. launch in 1H 2027, if approved. The FDA had previously granted lonvo-z a Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy (RMAT) designation.
